text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Margaret Chen
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]
SEX: Female
ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: [Thoracic Oncology]
SUBMITTING SERVICE: Interventional Radiology / Thoracic Oncology
CLINICAL HISTORY: 63-year-old female with known stage IIIB (T3N3M0) left lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR L858R-mutant, on osimertinib (Tagrisso) first-line therapy. CT imaging demonstrates new right adrenal nodule (1.1 cm) identified on restaging imaging in the setting of systemic disease progression (interval increase of primary left upper lobe lung mass, new left hilar lymphadenopathy). Biopsy performed to confirm metastatic disease and obtain tissue for molecular resistance profiling.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN SUBMITTED:
Part A: Right adrenal gland, CT-guided core needle biopsy (3 cores submitted in formalin)

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [DATE]
DATE OF RECEIPT: [DATE]
DATE OF FINAL REPORT: [DATE]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL DIAGNOSIS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

PART A — RIGHT ADRENAL GLAND, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:

METASTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, CONSISTENT WITH LUNG PRIMARY ORIGIN
(see comment and ancillary studies)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Histologic sections show cores of adrenal cortical tissue with infiltration by a malignant epithelial neoplasm. The tumor is composed of atypical glandular and acinar structures with moderately pleomorphic nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and moderate amounts of pale eosinophilic cytoplasm. Mitotic figures are readily identifiable. The neoplastic cells demonstrate focal cribriform architecture and individual tumor cell infiltration of the adrenal stromal compartment. No adrenal cortical or medullary differentiation is identified in the tumor component.

The morphologic features are those of a metastatic adenocarcinoma. The immunohistochemical profile (TTF-1 strongly positive, Napsin-A focally positive, CK7 positive, CK20 negative — see ancillary studies below) is consistent with a lung primary origin. Correlation with the known clinical history of left upper lobe lung adenocarcinoma is strongly supportive of this interpretation.

The morphology is similar to the prior diagnostic biopsy of the left upper lobe primary tumor (see Surgical Pathology Case [PRIOR ACCESSION NUMBER]), which demonstrated invasive adenocarcinoma with acinar predominant pattern and TTF-1/Napsin-A positivity.

Tissue has been triaged for molecular/genomic testing including comprehensive next-generation sequencing (NGS panel) and targeted EGFR T790M mutation analysis by cobas assay. Results will be reported under separate addendum when available.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on Part A, Block A1):

  TTF-1 (clone 8G7G3/1):           POSITIVE — strong, diffuse nuclear staining in neoplastic cells
  Napsin-A (polyclonal):            FOCALLY POSITIVE — granular cytoplasmic staining in approximately 20-30% of neoplastic cells
  CK7 (clone OV-TL 12/30):         POSITIVE — diffuse cytoplasmic staining in neoplastic cells
  CK20 (clone Ks20.8):             NEGATIVE — no staining in neoplastic cells
  p40 (clone BC28):                NEGATIVE — no nuclear staining in neoplastic cells (squamous excluded)
  Synaptophysin (clone SP11):      NEGATIVE — no staining in neoplastic cells (neuroendocrine excluded)
  Chromogranin A (polyclonal):     NEGATIVE — no staining in neoplastic cells
  SF-1 (clone N1665):              NEGATIVE — no staining in neoplastic cells (adrenocortical carcinoma excluded)
  Inhibin-alpha (clone R1):        NEGATIVE — no staining in neoplastic cells
  PAX8 (clone MRQ-50):             NEGATIVE — no nuclear staining in neoplastic cells (renal, thyroid, and Mullerian origins excluded)
  CDX2 (clone AMT28):              NEGATIVE — no nuclear staining (colorectal/GI origin excluded)

  Interpretation: The immunoprofile (TTF-1+, Napsin-A focally+, CK7+, CK20-, p40-, SF-1-, PAX8-) supports metastatic lung adenocarcinoma. The focal and reduced Napsin-A staining compared to primary lung tumors may reflect dedifferentiation in the metastatic setting.

MOLECULAR/GENOMIC STUDIES — PENDING:
  - Comprehensive next-generation sequencing (NGS): Tissue submitted; results pending. Report to be issued as addendum.
  - EGFR T790M targeted mutation analysis (cobas EGFR Mutation Test v2): Tissue submitted; results pending. Report to be issued as addendum.

Note: Specimen adequacy was confirmed by the performing pathologist prior to molecular triage. Estimated tumor cellularity: approximately 40-50% (sufficient for molecular testing).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Part A is received in formalin, labeled with the patient's name, medical record number, and designated "right adrenal biopsy." The specimen consists of three tan-pink, cylindrical core needle biopsy fragments measuring 0.9 cm, 0.7 cm, and 0.6 cm in greatest dimension (aggregate length approximately 2.2 cm) with a diameter of approximately 0.1 cm each. The cores are soft to slightly firm in consistency. No grossly visible hemorrhage or necrosis is identified macroscopically. All three cores are submitted entirely in one cassette (A1), wrapped in lens paper.

Representative sections examined: 1 cassette (A1: all cores, entirely submitted)
Total number of cassettes: 1

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Sections of the three biopsy cores show variable amounts of adrenal cortical parenchyma and fibrous stromal tissue admixed with infiltrating neoplastic glandular structures. The tumor is present in all three submitted cores. The neoplastic cells are arranged predominantly in irregular glands and acinar units, with focally solid nests and individual cell infiltration at the advancing edge. Tumor cell nuclei are enlarged, ovoid to irregular in contour, with vesicular chromatin and one to two conspicuous nucleoli. Mitotic figures number approximately 3-4 per 10 high-power fields. No definitive lymphovascular invasion is identified within the biopsy material; however, assessment is limited given the small volume of tissue. No necrosis is identified. The background non-neoplastic adrenal cortex shows mild lipid depletion, a nonspecific finding.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC SUMMARY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Metastatic adenocarcinoma involving the right adrenal gland, morphologically and immunohistochemically consistent with lung primary origin (TTF-1 positive, Napsin-A focally positive, CK7 positive; neuroendocrine, adrenocortical, renal, and gastrointestinal markers negative).

2. Molecular profiling (comprehensive NGS and EGFR T790M cobas assay) submitted from this specimen — RESULTS PENDING; addendum to follow.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Pathologist: [Attending Pathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
Electronically signed: [DATE]

Specimen Log: [ACCESSION NUMBER]
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*** ADDENDUM — TO BE ISSUED UPON RECEIPT OF MOLECULAR RESULTS ***
*** FINAL DIAGNOSIS SUBJECT TO REVISION PENDING MOLECULAR TESTING ***
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
